# growthbook_metric (Resource)

A GrowthBook (legacy) metric. Metrics define how experiment success is measured against a data source via a SQL query and analysis behavior settings. The `sqlBuilder` and `mixpanel` query styles are not managed by this resource; use the `sql` block to define the metric query.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "growthbook_metric" "signup" {
  name          = "Signups"
  type          = "binomial"
  datasource_id = "ds_warehouse"
  description   = "User completed signup"
  projects      = [growthbook_project.web.id]
  tags          = ["acquisition"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `datasource_id` (String) ID of the data source this metric queries. Changing this forces a new metric.
- `name` (String) Human-readable metric name.
- `type` (String) Metric type: `binomial`, `count`, `duration`, or `revenue`.

### Optional

- `archived` (Boolean) Whether the metric is archived.
- `behavior` (Attributes) Analysis behavior settings for the metric (goal direction, capping, conversion window, bayesian prior, thresholds). Server-applied defaults are not read back into state; only the values you configure are tracked. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--behavior))
- `description` (String) Optional description of the metric.
- `managed_by` (String) Where the metric is managed from: empty (anywhere) or `api`.
- `owner` (String) User ID or email of the metric owner.
- `projects` (List of String) Project IDs that can access this metric.
- `sql` (Attributes) SQL definition of the metric. Preferred over the unsupported `sqlBuilder`/`mixpanel` styles. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sql))
- `tags` (List of String) Tags applied to the metric.

### Read-Only

- `date_created` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `date_updated` (String) Last update timestamp.
- `id` (String) Unique metric identifier (e.g. `met_...`).

<a id="nestedatt--behavior"></a>
### Nested Schema for `behavior`

Optional:

- `capping_settings` (Attributes) Controls how metric outliers are capped. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--behavior--capping_settings))
- `goal` (String) Metric goal direction: `increase` or `decrease`.
- `max_percent_change` (Number) Maximum percent change to consider uplift significant (proportion).
- `min_percent_change` (Number) Minimum percent change to consider uplift significant (proportion).
- `min_sample_size` (Number) Minimum sample size before showing results.
- `prior_settings` (Attributes) Controls the bayesian prior for the metric. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--behavior--prior_settings))
- `risk_threshold_danger` (Number) Deprecated risk danger threshold (proportion).
- `risk_threshold_success` (Number) Deprecated risk success threshold (proportion).
- `target_mde` (Number) Target minimum detectable effect, as a proportion.
- `window_settings` (Attributes) Controls the conversion window for the metric. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--behavior--window_settings))

<a id="nestedatt--behavior--capping_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `behavior.capping_settings`

Optional:

- `ignore_zeros` (Boolean) When percentile capping, ignore zero values when computing the percentile.
- `type` (String) Capping type: `none`, `absolute`, or `percentile`.
- `value` (Number) Absolute cap value, or percentile (0.0-1.0) depending on `type`.


<a id="nestedatt--behavior--prior_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `behavior.prior_settings`

Optional:

- `mean` (Number) Mean of the prior distribution of relative effects (proportion).
- `override` (Boolean) If false, organization default prior settings are used.
- `proper` (Boolean) If true, use the `mean` and `stddev`; otherwise use an improper flat prior.
- `stddev` (Number) Standard deviation of the prior distribution (must be > 0).


<a id="nestedatt--behavior--window_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `behavior.window_settings`

Optional:

- `delay_unit` (String) Unit for `delay_value`: `minutes`, `hours`, `days`, or `weeks`.
- `delay_value` (Number) How long after exposure before counting conversions.
- `type` (String) Window type: `none`, `conversion`, or `lookback`.
- `window_unit` (String) Unit for `window_value`: `minutes`, `hours`, `days`, or `weeks`.
- `window_value` (Number) Length of the conversion window.



<a id="nestedatt--sql"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sql`

Optional:

- `conversion_sql` (String) SQL query returning conversion rows for the metric.
- `denominator_metric_id` (String) Denominator metric ID for funnel/ratio metrics.
- `identifier_types` (List of String) Identifier types this metric supports.
- `user_aggregation_sql` (String) Custom user-level aggregation SQL (default `SUM(value)`).
